fields.description: Master switch. When off, no duplicate checking happens at all.
fields.label: Enabled

fields.description: Reject duplicates with an error. This is the normal production behaviour.
fields.label: Strict Mode

fields.description: Do not block duplicates; instead create a Duplicate Report row for later review. Migration Mode overrides Strict Mode.
fields.label: Migration Mode

fields.description: Numeric dialing code without '+', used when a bare number has no country code. India = 91, USA/Canada = 1, UK = 44.
fields.label: Default Country Code

fields.description: ISO country code (2 letters) used to interpret numbers typed without a country code. India = IN, USA = US, UK = GB. Used by the phonenumbers library when installed.
fields.label: Default Region (ISO)

fields.description: Expected number of digits in a local number (India = 10).
fields.label: National Number Length

fields.description: One DocType per line. These are the DocTypes that get duplicate-checked. Address is intentionally excluded.
fields.label: Guarded DocTypes

fields.default:
Sales: Lead, Customer
Purchase: Supplier
HR: Employee
fields.description: Business functions. Duplicates are only detected WITHIN a function, so the same phone/email may appear across functions. Format 'Function: DocType, DocType', one per line. A Contact inherits the function of the party it links to.
fields.label: Function Scopes

fields.description: Employee statuses that count as currently employed, one per line. Name/phone/personal-email uniqueness is enforced ONLY against employees in these statuses, so a resigned person's details free up for reuse when the same person rejoins. ERPNext options: Active, Inactive, Suspended, Left.
fields.label: Active Employee Statuses

fields.description: Enforce unique names among active employees. Turn this OFF if two genuinely different employees may share a name (common in many regions); phone and personal email remain checked either way.
fields.label: Check Employee Names

fields.description: Your official company email domains, one per line (e.g. example.com). Addresses on these domains are NEVER checked for Employees, because an official mailbox is legitimately shared and gets reassigned from a leaver to a new hire. Every other (personal) employee email must still be unique. Leave blank to check all employee emails.
fields.label: Company Email Domains (Exempt)

fields.description: Phone/email/name fields are discovered automatically from DocType metadata. Use these only when the automatic detection gets it wrong on your site.
fields.label: Field Discovery Overrides

fields.description: Optional. Override which field(s) hold the entity name, one DocType per line, e.g. 'Lead: company_name, lead_name'. Leave blank to use sensible defaults.
fields.label: Name Field Overrides

fields.description: Optional. Declare phone fields explicitly, one DocType per line, e.g. 'Lead: mobile_no, custom_alt_number'. REPLACES automatic detection for that DocType.
fields.label: Phone Field Overrides

fields.description: Optional. Declare email fields explicitly, one DocType per line, e.g. 'Employee: personal_email'. REPLACES automatic detection for that DocType.
fields.label: Email Field Overrides

fields.description: Optional. Fields to exclude from checking, one DocType per line, e.g. 'Lead: email_signature'. Subtracted from whatever detection or an override produced.
fields.label: Ignored Fields
